agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New Express Delivery product offering premium time-bound delivery (target: 2 hours) at a surcharge, with a refund policy for missed delivery windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal stakeholders.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — a sub-domain of Order Fulfillment that introduces time-sensitive delivery commitments, driver eligibility constraints, and a conditional refund policy tied to delivery window compliance.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option with a target delivery window of 2 hours from order placement, available to eligible customers at a surcharge." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects Express Delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of delivery tier. Never refunded under the express refund policy." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour target timeframe within which an Express Delivery should be completed, starting from order placement." },
      { "term": "Express Eligibility (Customer)", "definition": "A customer qualifies for Express Delivery only if their delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse. Distance calculation method (radius vs driving distance) is currently undefined." },
      { "term": "Express Eligibility (Driver)", "definition": "A driver may fulfill Express Delivery orders only if their current rating is 4.0 stars or above." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The internal operational requirement that an Express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement." },
      { "term": "Driver Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute window within which a replacement driver must be assigned after a driver cancels an in-progress Express Delivery." },
      { "term": "Express Refund", "definition": "A goodwill credit of the Express Fee ($12.99) issued when the delivery window is missed, subject to exclusions. Not a contractual obligation." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exclusion", "definition": "An express refund exclusion applied when the delivery window was missed due to an incorrect address provided by the customer." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "The state when an Express Delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window from order placement." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express Delivery option must only be offered to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Offering express outside the serviceable radius makes the delivery window physically unachievable and corrupts the service promise. This is a hard geographic constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a customer address more than 10km from any warehouse, the Express Delivery option must not appear as selectable in the checkout flow."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The Express Fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The fee amount is a pricing policy decision that can be changed by business leadership without breaking system integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects Express Delivery, the order total must include exactly $12.99 as a separate Express Fee line item on top of the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above are eligible to be assigned Express Delivery orders.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Assigning an ineligible driver to an express order violates the operational quality constraint and undermines the service commitment. The threshold value (4.0) is policy, but the constraint that a threshold exists and is enforced at assignment time is an invariant.",
      "testable_assertion": "The dispatch system must never assign an Express Delivery order to a driver whose current rating is below the configured express eligibility threshold (currently 4.0)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "An Express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 15-minute window is an operational target. Missing it degrades service but does not corrupt system state. The threshold may be tuned.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver has picked up an Express order within 15 minutes of placement, an operational alert must be raised and the order must be flagged as at-risk."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the delivery window is missed on an Express Delivery, the Express Fee ($12.99) is refunded. The base delivery fee is never refunded under this policy.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The refund policy is a business decision and goodwill gesture (per Legal), not a data integrity constraint. Policy could change.",
      "testable_assertion": "When an Express Delivery is marked as delivered after the 2-hour window, a refund of exactly the Express Fee amount is initiated. The base delivery fee remains charged."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Express Fee refund is excluded when the missed window was caused by the customer providing an incorrect address.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a refund policy exclusion that could be modified by business decision.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a delivery window is missed AND the order is flagged with a 'wrong address' reason, no Express Fee refund is issued."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels an in-progress Express Delivery and no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes, an automatic refund of the Express Fee is triggered.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a customer protection policy, not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a driver cancels an Express Delivery and 10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver being assigned, the system automatically initiates an Express Fee refund without manual intervention."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Express Delivery refunds must be processed within 3 business days of the missed window event.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing timeline is a finance/operations policy, not a system invariant.",
      "testable_assertion": "All Express Fee refund records must have a processing completion date no later than 3 business days after the refund was initiated."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — must clarify whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive, and whether distance is Euclidean (radius) or road/driving distance." },
    { "scenario": "Driver's rating drops below 4.0 stars while carrying an active Express order", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — must clarify whether the eligibility check is only at assignment time or also enforced during delivery. Recommendation: check at assignment only; do not revoke mid-delivery." },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers are available when an Express order is placed", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — must clarify whether to reject the express option at checkout, queue the order, or fall back to standard delivery." },
    { "scenario": "Customer selects Express, then changes their address to one beyond 10km", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — must clarify whether express eligibility is re-validated on address change and what happens to the express fee." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 119 of the 2-hour window", "expected_behavior": "The 10-minute replacement window extends past the 2-hour delivery window. Must clarify: does the customer get an automatic refund at the 2-hour mark, or does the system wait the full 10 minutes for a replacement?" },
    { "scenario": "Multiple drivers cancel sequentially on the same Express order", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does each cancellation reset the 10-minute replacement clock? At what point does the system give up and auto-refund?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides a subtly wrong address (e.g., wrong apartment number) that causes delay but not total failure", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — who determines 'wrong address'? What is the evidentiary standard? Driver's word alone could be abused." },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed 1 minute before warehouse closing time", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — no operating hours for Express have been specified." },
    { "scenario": "Customer is unavailable at delivery address within the 2-hour window", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does the window still apply? Is refund eligibility affected by customer unavailability?" }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express Fee ($12.99) creates a new revenue stream, but the refund policy introduces refund exposure on every missed window. The 'wrong address' exclusion mitigates some fraud/abuse risk. The 3-business-day refund processing window provides cash flow buffer. Unclear refund trigger automation (manual vs automatic) could create inconsistent costs.",
    "operational": "The 15-minute pickup SLA and 10-minute replacement window create tight operational constraints requiring real-time dispatch optimization. Driver eligibility filtering (4.0+ stars) reduces the available driver pool for express orders. Real-time tracking adds infrastructure requirements.",
    "user": "Prominent display of a '2-hour guarantee' that Legal says cannot be a guarantee creates customer expectation management risk. Real-time tracking is a strong UX differentiator. Automatic refunds on driver cancellation build trust. However, 'wrong address' exclusion disputes will generate support tickets.",
    "risk": "HIGH — The fundamental contradiction between the marketed 'guarantee' and Legal's 'best effort' classification is the single largest business risk. Customers who see 'guaranteed' and are denied refunds due to exclusions will escalate aggressively. The lack of a clear 'wrong address' determination process creates fraud and dispute risk on both sides.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium delivery option with time commitment and refund safety net. Risk of unmet expectations if 'guarantee' language is used but not legally enforceable." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "New order tier with tighter pickup SLAs. Only 4.0+ rated drivers eligible, creating a two-tier driver system. Cancellation triggers replacement clock, adding pressure." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "Must support 15-minute pickup readiness for express orders. May need priority picking/packing lanes." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes around 'wrong address' exclusions, missed window refund timing, and the gap between 'guaranteed' marketing and 'best effort' legal reality." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "Must process refunds within 3 business days. Needs reporting on express refund rate to monitor margin erosion." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must review all customer-facing language to ensure no contractual guarantee is implied. Refund framed as goodwill, not obligation." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 35,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is very low due to a fundamental unresolved contradiction at the core of the product: the PM and Customer Success describe a 'guarantee' while Legal explicitly prohibits guaranteeing delivery time. This is not a minor detail — it determines the entire product positioning, refund policy framing, and customer-facing language. Additionally, several critical operational flows are undefined (no-driver-available, wrong address adjudication, operating hours). The acceptance criteria cannot be finalized until the guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is resolved.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express Fee amount ($12.99) is clearly stated by Finance",
      "10km proximity requirement is a hard geographic constraint",
      "4.0 star driver eligibility threshold is clearly stated by Operations",
      "Base delivery fee is never refunded under express policy"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour commitment is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — directly contradicted",
      "How 'wrong address' is determined and by whom",
      "Whether refunds are automatic or manual (PM/CS imply automatic, Finance implies manual processing)",
      "What happens when no express-eligible drivers are available",
      "Whether distance is Euclidean radius or driving distance",
      "Operating hours and availability windows for Express"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time",
      "Driver rating is checked at assignment time, not continuously during delivery",
      "The 10km distance check uses the nearest warehouse, not a specific warehouse",
      "Express Delivery is an add-on to existing delivery, not a separate order flow"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee' to be shown prominently to customers. Legal explicitly states this CANNOT be a guarantee in writing — it is a 'best effort' SLA and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' This contradiction is foundational: it affects product positioning, customer-facing copy, refund policy framing, and potential legal liability. These two positions cannot coexist.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal/Compliance", "Customer Support", "Marketing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Frame as 'Express Target' with best-effort language and goodwill refund — aligns with Legal but weakens the value proposition",
        "B: Frame as a 'Service Level Commitment' with refund-backed promise — middle ground that implies accountability without contractual guarantee",
        "C: Frame as a hard guarantee with contractual backing — requires Legal to accept liability exposure"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B — Use language like '2-Hour Express Target' with a clear refund policy framed as a 'service commitment' rather than a legal guarantee. The refund policy itself provides de facto guarantee behavior without contractual liability. Legal must review and approve final wording."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Refund trigger mechanism is unclear. PM implies refund on any missed window. Customer Success says automatic refund on driver cancellation without replacement. Finance describes a 3-business-day processing timeline suggesting manual/batch processing. It is unclear whether refunds are (a) automatically triggered by system events, (b) manually reviewed and approved, or (c) a mix of both depending on the trigger.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance", "Customer Support", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: All express refunds are automatic — system detects missed window or replacement failure and issues refund",
        "B: All express refunds are manually reviewed within 3 business days",
        "C: Automatic trigger with 3-business-day processing/settlement window"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — Automatic system trigger (for auditability and customer trust) with a processing/settlement window of up to 3 business days (for Finance's cash flow and fraud review needs). This reconciles all three stakeholder positions."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 'wrong address' refund exclusion has no defined adjudication process. Who determines the address was wrong? What evidence is required? Can the customer dispute this determination? Without a clear process, this exclusion will become a source of customer disputes and inconsistent support decisions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Support", "Customers", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver flags wrong address, customer support reviews with evidence (photos, GPS)",
        "B: System detects address discrepancy automatically (e.g., GPS mismatch at delivery)",
        "C: Customer self-reports address correction, which retroactively disqualifies refund eligibility"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A with system assistance — Driver flags the issue, system captures GPS evidence, and Customer Support makes final determination. Provides human oversight while reducing fraud."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No operating hours or availability windows for Express Delivery are defined. Can customers order Express at 2 AM? On holidays? During severe weather events? Without this, the system cannot properly validate express eligibility at order time.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Warehouse Operations", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Express available 24/7 wherever warehouse is open",
        "B: Express available during defined business hours only",
        "C: Express availability is dynamic based on driver supply and warehouse capacity"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — Dynamic availability based on real-time operational capacity. This prevents offering Express when it cannot be fulfilled, but requires implementation of a capacity/availability check."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The 10km distance constraint does not specify whether this is Euclidean (straight-line) distance or actual driving/road distance. In urban environments with rivers, highways, or one-way systems, these can differ by 2-3x. This directly affects which customers see the Express option.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Engineering", "Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: Euclidean radius — simpler to compute, more generous eligibility",
        "B: Driving distance — more accurate to actual delivery feasibility",
        "C: Driving time estimate (e.g., 20 min) instead of distance — most operationally meaningful"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — A time-based threshold (e.g., estimated 20-minute drive) is the most operationally honest measure since the constraint exists to make the 2-hour window achievable. However, this requires routing/ETA infrastructure."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot define acceptance criteria while the guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is unresolved", "business_rule_broken": "The core product promise is undefined — every downstream criterion (refund triggers, customer-facing language, SLA monitoring) depends on this resolution." }
  ]
}
